note: The actual discoverable graph: named nodes at axis-coordinates, at every purity tier, with edges. This is the map-as-KG made concrete. The Valley is the anchor node (near origin, Tier-2). Nodes fog in until reached/heard. Engine derives adjacency + purity from axisVector; edges here are authored hints for the near-Valley subgraph. Coordinates are the 12 axes in order: [emotional_logical, falsehood_truth, demonic_angelic, violence_peace, concrete_abstract, mechanical_spiritual, chaos_order, dark_light, death_life, space_time, body_mind, destruction_creation]. Authored by Aevi (PO).

nodes.name: The Valley of Echoes
nodes.summary: The starting triad basin. Habitable, layered, near the origin. Everything the player knows at first is here or one edge out.

nodes.name: The Salt Meadows
nodes.summary: A gentle mixing-ground of pale grass and shallow salt-pans just down-valley — ordinary life, low extremity, a place people go to NOT have an adventure. Farmers, saltcutters, a slow river. The tutorial of peace. What the Valley protects.

nodes.name: The Quickwood Eaves
nodes.summary: The near edge of the death/life Reach, up the life-gradient from Thornwake Glade. Growth turns aggressive here — the forest arranges itself, wounds close overnight, and staying too long means the wood starts to keep you. First real taste of a pole's pull. Vivimancy can be learned at its edge; the Rootkin trade here.

nodes.name: The Hollow March
nodes.summary: The death-pole side of the same Reach, across the March from the Quickwood. Where growth stops. The Ashwardens tend endings here; grief-work and Palework are learned, and the ground itself is patient. Not evil — necessary, and getting stronger than it should.

nodes.name: The Lattice Frontier
nodes.summary: Up the order-gradient toward the Grand Lattice — the outermost ordered arcologies, where the roads are perfect and nothing is unplanned. The Lattice is expanding toward the Valley and calling it safety. Latticework is learned here; so is the unease of watching perfect order eat a free place. A preview of what the sealed thing could become.

nodes.name: The Churn Edge
nodes.summary: The opposite direction — down the chaos-gradient into the Unspooling, reached over the pass from the Roost. Territory that never maps the same twice; the Churnfolk ride luck and improvisation as a discipline. Wildcraft is learned here. Dangerous in a way that's the exact inverse of the Lattice's danger.

nodes.name: The Gearlands Verge
nodes.summary: Down the mechanical-gradient from the Archive — where Precursor mechanism thickens into machine-country that runs without people. Enginecraft is learned here. This is the near slope of the gradient whose far peak is What Sleeps Under. The water crisis is a tremor rolling down THIS hill.
nodes.hooks: GM-EYES-ONLY: primary approach to the mechanical_spiritual pole and the deep arc. Corvane the Deep Warden ranges here.

nodes.name: The Numen Threshold
nodes.summary: Up the spiritual-gradient from the Choir — thinning lands where the material world becomes negotiable and pure-meaning practice begins. The Numenwork disciplines (the mystic crafts the framework keeps converging toward) are learned here. Ledda the Last Choirmistress came back from further up this slope than anyone.

nodes.name: The Heartroot
nodes.summary: FOGGED FAR-NODE. The life-pole itself. Pure unstoppable growth. Rumored, not reached. To arrive unready is to be grown-into. The single biggest center of life in the world.

nodes.name: The Great Engine
nodes.summary: FOGGED FAR-NODE. The mechanical-pole. A machine-continent the size of a sea, running some purpose no one living knows. Deep up this gradient is where What Sleeps Under has its home. The far peak of the Valley's own crisis.

nodes.name: The Blaze
nodes.summary: FOGGED FAR-NODE. The light-pole. Total revelation that has burned its land to glass; nothing hidden, nothing shaded, nothing else alive. What the Radiant Plateau's High Luminary is a gentle foothill of — and what her 'unify and seal' would tend toward at full strength.

nodes.name: The Still Origin
nodes.summary: FOGGED. The still point — all axes zero. The blandest, safest, most characterless ground in the world. Nothing is strong; nothing is dangerous; nothing grows; nothing means much. Some flee here for peace and find the un-propagated life the framework warns of. A PLACE, not a metaphor — and a real temptation.

edges_note: Authored near-Valley edges are in each node's reachedFrom/edgeCost. The engine builds the full adjacency graph from axisVector distance: places within a short axis-distance are walkable neighbors; large distances are Marches (long/far edges) requiring real journeys. Discovery fogs all but the anchor until reached or clearly heard-of.
gradient_summary: From the Valley, the walkable gradients are: DOWN to Salt Meadows and the Still Origin (safety/blandness); UP the life-gradient through the Quickwood to the Heartroot; ACROSS to the Hollow March (death); toward ORDER via the Lattice Frontier; toward CHAOS via the Churn Edge (over the pass); DOWN the mechanical slope through the Gearlands Verge toward the Great Engine (the deep arc); UP the spiritual slope through the Numen Threshold; toward LIGHT via the Plateau to the Blaze. Each gradient teaches its craft and raises its peril. The player's history becomes a path drawn through the coordinate-space — their character literally IS the coordinates they've dared to hold.
